title: A2A Message
description: Wire format for a single A2A message between two agents. Persisted by the a2a-orchestrator MCP server to Mnemos. Consumed by the target agent's runtime as system-context supplement. Universal — works with any kebab-case agent id.

schema_version.description: Pin to a specific protocol version. Bump the constant and ship a migration if you change the wire format.

session_id.description: Mnemos session id this message belongs to. Multiple messages share a session_id; they form the chain.

from.description: A2A id of the sending agent (from its Agent Card).

signature.description: Optional Ed25519 signature (base64) over canonical JSON of the message. Required when the sender's Agent Card has a public_key. Verified by R6.

tenant_id.description: Optional tenant id for multi-tenant isolation. Defaults to 'default' when omitted.

to.description: A2A id of the intended receiving agent.

intent.description: Why this message exists. Drives the target agent's response style and the loop/budget rules.

reason.description: Human-readable one-paragraph explanation of why I'm handing off. Becomes the visible label in the user's UI when the switch happens.

summary.description: Short summary of what the sender has done/found. Replaces full chat history. Target agent uses this as system context.

key_decisions.description: Bullet list of decisions made by the sender. Helps target agent avoid re-litigating.

open_questions.description: Bullet list of things the target agent needs to resolve.

pointer.description: Either a file path (relative to repo) or a memory:// URI pointing to Mnemos turn.

artifacts.description: Pointers to artifacts the sender produced. Target agent may load_context() on them if needed.

chain.description: Ordered list of A2A ids already in this chain (sender included). Used by loop-controller.

depth.description: How deep this message is in the chain. 0 = first A2A hop.

calls_remaining.description: Budget counter. 3 - depth = remaining. When 0, MCP rejects further A2A from this chain.

parent_message_id.description: message_id of the message that triggered this one. Forms a tree if branches happen.

saga_id.description: Optional saga id for long-lived dialog state spanning multiple chains.
